description_long: This is a cylindrical stone water well with a thick, stacked stone wall forming the main body. The well features a wooden frame mounted on top, supporting a horizontal wooden spindle with a hand crank on one side and a rope wound around it. A wooden bucket is attached to the rope, designed for drawing water from the well. The stonework is roughly hewn, with visible mortar lines and a slightly irregular surface, giving it a rustic appearance. The wood components, including the spindle, crank, and bucket, are light in color and show a natural grain. The well is open at the top, revealing a dark water surface inside.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden crank mechanism and bucket, featuring a thick stone wall and open water surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden crank, spindle, and bucket hanging over the opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape, the wooden spindle from the end, and the bucket hanging to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with the crank handle and spindle visible, bucket hanging inside the well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the spindle end and bucket resting on the stone edge.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden spindle with a hand crank and rope mechanism is present, with a wooden bucket attached for drawing water.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a thick, cylindrical stone wall approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
